relatedness_reasoning: Both files involve customer arrears/financial difficulty data flowing between credit and customer_services systems; inbound arrears_status triggers outbound hardship_request. Key fields (customerId, accountNumber, arrearsAmount) map clearly, and the message flow (credit→customer_services→credit) suggests a plausible two-step process where arrears data informs hardship assistance requests.

mappings.reasoning: No equivalent identifier in inbound; likely system-generated for the outbound request.
Both are ISO8601 timestamps; outbound timestamp likely derived from or close to inbound message timestamp.
Inbound destinationSystem is CUSTOMER_SERVICES, which becomes the source_department in outbound; represents the system that generates the hardship request.
Inbound sourceSystem is CREDIT_DEPARTMENT, which becomes the target_department in outbound; represents the system receiving the hardship request.
Direct match: customerId from inbound arrears_status array maps to customer_id in outbound.
No customer name field exists in inbound schema; likely populated from external customer master data or user input.
Same semantic meaning (account identifier) but outbound format is IBAN while inbound is generic account_number; likely a transformation or validation step.
Hardship request type is not present in inbound arrears_status; this is a constant or system-determined value for the outbound message.
Inbound notes field may contain context about arrears; outbound hardship_reason likely extracted or inferred from notes, though not a perfect match.
Inbound riskLevel (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) correlates with hardship severity assessment; HIGH risk likely maps to high severity hardship.
No income data in inbound arrears_status; must come from customer-provided hardship application data or external source.
No expense data in inbound arrears_status; must come from customer-provided hardship application data or external source.
Inbound arrearsAmount represents outstanding arrears; outbound outstanding_balance likely derived from or equivalent to this field.
No contractual payment amount in inbound arrears_status; likely sourced from account/product master data or hardship application.
Relief type options (payment_reduction, payment_deferral) are not present in inbound; must come from customer hardship request form.
Duration of relief requested is not in inbound arrears_status; must come from customer hardship application.
Supporting document filenames are not present in inbound arrears_status; must come from hardship application submission.
No phone contact in inbound arrears_status; likely from customer master data or hardship application.
No email contact in inbound arrears_status; likely from customer master data or hardship application.
Inbound actionRequired flag may influence priority assignment; if true, could trigger normal or higher priority, though mapping is speculative.
Outbound status 'pending_credit_review' is a workflow state for the hardship request, not derivable from inbound arrears_status.
